message:
fix(func): compute UTC offset per-datetime to handle DST transitions correctly

The localtime/utc datetime modifiers previously computed the system UTC
offset once at startup (via `date +%z`, cached in OnceLock) and applied
the same fixed offset to all conversions. This caused 1-hour errors for
datetimes in the opposite DST phase from when the process started.

Replace the cached offset with per-call computation using chrono::Local,
which resolves the correct offset (including DST) for each specific
datetime being converted — matching C SQLite's localtime_r() behaviour.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
